description: A graph-based section engine over the proof dependency DAG: a ProofTree with antichain / maximal-antichain checks and a frontier abstraction (F_named/F_size/F_namespace), implementing decomposition policies pi_root (rough - haves inlined) and pi_leaf (fine - haves named), plus pi_depth/pi_size/pi_reuse/pi_random. Policies are graph-only and origin-agnostic.
motivation: Operationalize 'decomposition' as a cut of the proof poset so granularity becomes a controllable variable.
formal_results: Implemented with a passing unit-test suite (graph properties, all policies, frontiers, origin-agnosticism).
results_description: A formal, tested section-selection engine; pi_root and pi_leaf are the two primary granularity policies.
conclusions: A theorem can be re-articulated at multiple granularities by choosing a section (maximal antichain) of its bounded-unfolded dependency DAG.

comments.caveat: Dependency edges are currently TYPE-level only (Stage 0b v1, usesConsts/usesHyps = typeDeps); proof-term-level dependencies are not yet captured, so section antichains may be too sparse - a flagged soundness caveat (STAGE0B_DESIGN.md).
